authoritative_sources.title: 42 CFR Part 484 - Home Health Services
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Federal home-health conditions of participation define the regulated baseline for home-health service delivery, care planning, coordination, quality, records, and patient-rights expectations that constrain home-services analytics.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: home-health service model, evidence prerequisites, and regulated boundary review
used_for: field service and documentation evidence expectations

authoritative_sources.title: Home Health Agencies - Conditions of Participation
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS publishes the official home-health condition-of-participation operating baseline and related survey/certification references for HHA quality and evidence expectations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: home-health service quality, documentation, and survey-readiness baseline

authoritative_sources.title: Medicare Benefit Policy Manual, Chapter 7 - Home Health Services
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public payer or provider operating guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS Medicare benefit policy is the authoritative public payer baseline for Medicare home-health benefit prerequisites, certification context, service coverage concepts, and plan-of-care dependencies.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: eligibility, certification, service prerequisite, and benefit-context review
used_for: boundary against medical-necessity or payer interpretation

authoritative_sources.title: Medicare Claims Processing Manual, Chapter 10 - Home Health Agency Billing
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public payer or provider operating guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS claims-processing manuals are the authoritative public Medicare baseline for HHA billing, claim-state, payment, remittance, denial, and revenue-cycle terminology used in analytical source reconciliation.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: charge, claim, remittance, denial, and appeal evidence review

authoritative_sources.title: CMS Interoperability and Prior Authorization Final Rule
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public payer or provider operating guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS prior-authorization guidance is authoritative for payer and provider prior-authorization process, response, and data-access expectations that can gate home-services analytics.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: authorization dependency, payer response, and data-access review

authoritative_sources.title: Minimum Necessary Requirement
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Health and Human Services
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official HHS HIPAA guidance is authoritative for limiting PHI in analytical reports, examples, service notes, exception registers, and escalation packets.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: PHI, minimum-necessary, example, and audience-control review

authoritative_sources.title: Home Health Quality Reporting Program
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS Home Health Quality Reporting Program is the official measure and reporting baseline for home-health quality metrics and public-reporting context.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: home-health quality measure, reporting, and public-comparison review

authoritative_sources.title: OASIS Data and Measure Information
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS OASIS data specifications are the official baseline for home-health assessment data items, measure logic inputs, and assessment-based analytics evidence.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: OASIS assessment data, measure input, and artifact-normalization review
used_for: OASIS item and assessment status handling

authoritative_sources.title: Home Health Star Ratings
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS Home Health Star Ratings provide official public-reporting and comparability context for home-health quality and patient-experience metrics.

authoritative_sources.title: United States Core Data for Interoperability
authoritative_sources.publisher: Office of the National Coordinator for Health Information Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: ONC USCDI is the official interoperability baseline for healthcare data classes used to trace patient, encounter, problem, procedure, order, and observation evidence in analytics.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: interoperability terminology and clinical-administrative record lineage
used_for: boundary against clinical interpretation through data mapping

workflow_stages.name: service request intake and prerequisite framing
workflow_stages.description: Confirm request intent, audience, program, payer, plan, provider type, service setting, reporting period, patient/member context, service dates, referral, authorization, eligibility, privacy constraints, source hierarchy, and retrieval hooks before resolving the analytics request.
source_classes: Public payer or provider operating guidance

workflow_stages.name: home-service metric definition and denominator selection
workflow_stages.description: Define home-services metrics, numerator, denominator, exclusions, attribution, threshold posture, refresh window, baseline comparison, and tenant adaptation rules for referral, start-of-care, visits, OASIS, quality, denial, remittance, appeal, and SLA measures.
source_classes: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
Public payer or provider operating guidance

workflow_stages.name: record retrieval and cross-system validation
workflow_stages.description: Reconcile EHR/EMR, clinical documentation, practice management, payer portal, revenue cycle, case management, utilization review, CRM, patient/member access, OASIS, quality reporting, and BI records to determine whether the service request can be resolved confidently.
source_classes: Public payer or provider operating guidance
Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance

workflow_stages.name: analytical brief and stakeholder resolution assembly
workflow_stages.description: Produce resolution notes, stakeholder communication summaries, analytical briefs, source-lineage maps, caveat statements, data-quality exceptions, and action-threshold guidance without overstating authority.
source_classes: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance

workflow_stages.name: privacy, stronger-claim, and authority review
workflow_stages.description: Check PHI, minimum-necessary, low-volume, patient-safety, clinical, payer, public-reporting, policy-exception, and unsupported trend or forecast risks before communicating or closing the request.
source_classes: Public payer or provider operating guidance
Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance

workflow_stages.name: exception escalation and adjacent-owner handoff
workflow_stages.description: Route policy exceptions, unresolved source conflicts, repeat data failures, PHI handling gaps, clinical or patient-safety questions, payer interpretation, coding, denial, appeal, quality attestation, and executive disclosure approval to the correct adjacent owner with evidence preserved.
source_classes: Public payer or provider operating guidance

artifacts: service request or CRM ticket with request summary, audience, cadence, and intended use
tenant metric dictionary, threshold rules, source-precedence policy, approval matrix, and publication posture
eligibility, benefits, referral, prior authorization, payer portal, and patient/member access records
EHR or EMR encounter, order, plan-of-care reference, field clinician note, visit schedule, missed-visit reason, and discharge or care-transition records
OASIS assessment extracts, home-health quality measure files, HHCAHPS or Care Compare context, and quality-reporting evidence
charge capture, coding, 837 claim, 835 remittance, denial, CARC/RARC, appeal, and payment documentation
case management, utilization review, CRM, stakeholder communication, and service-resolution notes
source-lineage map, reconciliation note, data-quality exception register, PHI handling note, and escalation package
systems_of_record: EHR or EMR and associated clinical documentation systems
home-health field visit, scheduling, and clinician documentation systems
practice management, revenue cycle, billing, claims, remittance, denial, and appeal systems
payer portal and prior authorization systems
case management, utilization review, CRM, service desk, and patient/member access systems
enterprise data warehouse, governed BI, metric dictionary, and data catalog
consent, privacy, disclosure, audit-log, and approval-owner repositories

allowed: Classify the request as in-scope, out-of-scope, or blocked on missing prerequisites.
Request or validate the minimum tenant facts, authoritative records, and source context required to continue safely.
Determine whether the analytics service request can be resolved directly, needs clarification, or must be escalated.
Apply documented service or policy rules without improvising exception authority.
Choose the source hierarchy, metric definitions, denominator and exclusion logic, refresh assumptions, caveat posture, and action thresholds that govern the analysis.
Prepare resolution notes, stakeholder communication summaries, analytical briefs, source-lineage maps, exception notes, and escalation packages without overstating authority.
Flag when trend conclusions, benchmarks, forecasts, or stronger claims are not supported by the underlying records.
escalate: Missing eligibility, referral, authorization, patient/member status, service dates, payer, plan, provider type, service setting, OASIS, quality, source hierarchy, privacy, or retrieval facts that would change the answer
Conflicting EHR, field-note, OASIS, claim, remittance, denial, appeal, CRM, or BI sources that materially change a metric outcome
PHI handling, minimum-necessary, low-volume, consent, disclosure, privacy, or patient/member safety uncertainty

Requests requiring clinical review, medical necessity, payer interpretation, coding decision, appeal strategy, quality attestation, policy exception, financial approval, or executive disclosure signoff
Repeat data-quality or source-owner failure that prevents supported resolution after one retrieval pass
refuse: Requests to override policy, pricing, eligibility, authorization, coverage, service level, or payer limits to resolve a case
Requests to diagnose, plan treatment, determine medical necessity, approve patient-safety posture, or provide licensed clinical judgment
Requests to reinterpret payer policy, approve coding or appeals, determine coverage, or certify quality/public reporting
Requests to expose PHI or patient/member-level examples without minimum-necessary and approval context
Requests to publish unsupported causal, benchmark, forecast, public-facing, or certainty claims

domain_failure_modes: Treating a home-services CRM ticket as sufficient evidence when EHR, field-note, plan-of-care, authorization, referral, eligibility, OASIS, or claim records are missing.
Collapsing EHR encounter, OASIS, charge, claim, remittance, denial, appeal, CRM, and BI outputs into one metric despite different lag, owner, and truth-set rules.
Inferring payer coverage, authorization status, medical necessity, coding correction, or appeal strategy from an analytics trend.
Publishing PHI, low-volume examples, patient/member-level detail, or clinician-level detail without minimum-necessary basis and approval context.
Using home-services analytics to imply clinical safety, diagnosis, treatment planning, or plan-of-care changes.
Changing numerator, denominator, exclusions, or service-level status to satisfy a stakeholder policy exception.
Presenting internal home-services trends as benchmark, public-reporting, quality, outcome, or forecast claims without formal measure logic and signoff.
Failing to escalate repeat source conflicts or missing retrieval hooks, causing operators to act on low-confidence reports.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Missing authorization, referral, or eligibility dependency
PHI or privacy-sensitive request that requires controlled handling
Boundary case that would require licensed clinical or payer-interpretation escalation
Customer or stakeholder requests a policy exception.
Service request lacks the detail needed for safe resolution.
Communication becomes a handoff to another lane.
Different systems disagree on the metric outcome.
A stakeholder asks for stronger conclusions than the data supports.
Request for unsupported workaround or discretionary exception.
Scenario proving coverage, authorization, referral, or documentation prerequisite handling where applicable.
Scenario proving safe escalation when patient safety, clinical judgment, or licensed review is required.
Normal in-scope execution using realistic industry terminology and artifact shapes.
Boundary rejection when the request should move to an adjacent specialist.
Low-confidence handling when required facts, records, or authority sources are missing.
Conflicting-source or conflicting-policy handling that requires escalation instead of confident synthesis.
Tenant-specific adaptation handling that preserves the public baseline and records assumptions.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant-specific home-services metric dictionaries and payer rules
unresolved_ambiguities.detail: Home-services tenants vary on start-of-care, missed-visit, authorization turnaround, OASIS completeness, denial, referral, and SLA definitions by payer, product, provider type, service setting, and contract. The package treats these as runtime prerequisites and requires explicit tenant mapping before resolution.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Jurisdictional privacy, licensure, and payer-program differences
unresolved_ambiguities.detail: State law, payer contracts, Medicaid programs, accreditation rules, and tenant policy can be stricter than the federal public baseline. The package escalates when local privacy, licensed review, payer interpretation, public-reporting, or patient-safety authority changes the answer.

notes: Authoritative home-services analytics records are the tenant-designated EHR, field-visit, OASIS, quality, referral, authorization, eligibility, practice management, revenue-cycle, claim, remittance, denial, appeal, payer, CRM, case-management, privacy, and governed BI systems constrained by the cited public guidance.

The lane stops before diagnosis, treatment planning, medical necessity, payer-policy interpretation, coding-policy interpretation, privacy-law interpretation, quality attestation, public disclosure approval, regulatory submission, authorization approval, financial approval, and patient-safety judgment.
No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for validated specification-based packaging because tenant variability is handled through runtime retrieval, caveat, refusal, and escalation rules.
